propositions: Repeated failed rebuilds accumulate multi-GB buildkit cache and dangling layers until the root disk hits ENOSPC and fail-closes the LLM-Review gate.
Docker cache pruning previously ran only on the deploy success path and never pruned the buildkit build cache on either path.
A helper prune_docker_caches caps the buildkit cache via docker builder prune --keep-storage (default 5GB, tunable via BUILD_CACHE_KEEP) and removes dangling images.
Prune invocations must be best-effort and non-fatal so they never mask the deploy-failure exit code, but log a non-fatal warning on failure.

The prune helper is called from record_backoff_failure and the success path so every failed and successful attempt reclaims disk, with each prune wrapped in timeout 120 to avoid holding the deploy lock.
